diagnosis.root_cause: No caching bug. The near-zero percent changes are real physics: the smooth Gaussian IC converges spectrally by N~48, so E_total(T=0) is identical at N=64 and N=128 to machine precision. Binding energies inherit this convergence for mild-interaction configs. High-cross-edge configs (cube polarized T1) show 73% magnitude change due to amplified nonlinear dynamics, but the sign is still preserved.

rerun.caching_fix_applied: None needed -- no caching bug found

verdict.notes: All N=128 files are genuine computations verified by: (1) trajectory divergence from N=64 after T=0, (2) energy drift 30-40x smaller at N=128, (3) runtime 8-10x longer. Magnitude convergence fails for cube polarized T1 (73% change) but the sign pattern -- the key observable for the selection rule -- is 100% robust. The two KEY TESTS (cube checkerboard E_bind=-3.86, ico ce_15_A E_bind=+3.56) both preserve their signs with <0.02% magnitude change, indicating these binding energies are well-converged.
